<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor cols"><div class="col"><pre><code><span class="com">// ❌ BEFORE: instanceof chains to fork behavior</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">describe</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">animal</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">if</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">instanceof</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Dog</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;Woof&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">}</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">else</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">if</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">instanceof</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Cat</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;Meow&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">}</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">else</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">if</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">instanceof</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Cow</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;Moo&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">throw</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">new</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">IllegalArgumentException</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;Unknown animal&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="com">// Add an animal =&gt; you must edit this method</span></code></pre></div><span class="col-divider"></span><div class="col"><pre><code><span class="com">// ✅ AFTER: let each type own its behavior</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">interface</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">sound</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Dog</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">implements</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">sound</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;Woof&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Cat</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">implements</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">sound</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;Meow&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Cow</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">implements</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">sound</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;Moo&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="com">// Call site: no type checks, no casts</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">describe</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">animal</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">animal</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">sound</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="com">// Add an animal =&gt; just add a class (open/closed)</span></code></pre></div></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡Java: With polymorphism, you can replace the use of instanceof in your code as a tool to fork logic. Let objects decide their behavior. #Java #OO

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #66

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// One line replaces ~40 lines of boilerplate</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">record</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">Point</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="kw">int</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">x</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">int</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">y</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="com">// Auto-generated: constructor, accessors, equals, hashCode, toString</span>
<span class="typ">Point</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">p</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">new</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">Point</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="num">3</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="num">4</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="kw">int</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">x</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">p</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">x</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip💡: Starting from Java 14 you can use "record" to create short, immutable, data-carrying objects. ✅ They are shorter than ordinary POJOs ✅ They have built-in equals(), hashCode(), toString() ✅ They are immutable by default #Java #Records

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  margin: 0;

  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;

.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;

}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;

  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;

.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;

  font-size: 16px;

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// ⚠️ Compiles fine — warnings (if enabled) flag the real problems:</span>
<span class="typ">List</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">list</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">new</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">ArrayList</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt">          </span><span class="com">// [rawtypes] raw type</span>
<span class="txt">list</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">add</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;hello&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">Integer</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">n</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Integer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">list</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">get</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="num">0</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt">    </span><span class="com">// [unchecked] unsafe cast → ClassCastException at runtime</span>

<span class="com">// ✅ Fix the code — generics remove the cast, so the bug can&#x27;t happen</span>
<span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">safe</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">new</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">ArrayList</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">safe</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">add</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;hello&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">s</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">safe</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">get</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="num">0</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt">               </span><span class="com">// type-safe, no cast needed</span>

<span class="com">// --- Make warnings fail the build so the ⚠️ block never ships ---</span>

<span class="com">// Maven (pom.xml)</span>
<span class="com">// &lt;plugin&gt;</span>
<span class="com">//   &lt;artifactId&gt;maven-compiler-plugin&lt;/artifactId&gt;</span>
<span class="com">//   &lt;configuration&gt;</span>
<span class="com">//     &lt;compilerArgs&gt;</span>
<span class="com">//       &lt;arg&gt;-Xlint:all&lt;/arg&gt;</span>
<span class="com">//       &lt;arg&gt;-Werror&lt;/arg&gt;</span>
<span class="com">//     &lt;/compilerArgs&gt;</span>
<span class="com">//   &lt;/configuration&gt;</span>
<span class="com">// &lt;/plugin&gt;</span>

<span class="com">// javac:  javac -Xlint:all -Werror *.java</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip #14💡: Enable compiler warnings and treat them as errors. They help prevent bugs early. #Java #BestPractices

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">private</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">static</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Logger</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">log</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">LoggerFactory</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getLogger</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">OrderService</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="txt">log</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">info</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;Created order {} for {}&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">orderId</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="com">// Lazy formatting, log levels, no System.out</span></code></pre></div></div>

  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}

  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;

.carbon {

  font-size: 16px;
}

<span class="com">// #ModernJava #DevTips</span></code></pre></div></div>

Tweet #70

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java Tip #17💡: Avoid public fields. Use private fields with getters/setters for encapsulation. #Java #OOP</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip #17💡: Avoid public fields. Use private fields with getters/setters for encapsulation. #Java #OOP

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">private</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">static</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Logger</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">log</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">LoggerFactory</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getLogger</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">OrderService</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="txt">log</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">info</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;Created order {} for {}&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">orderId</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="com">// Lazy formatting, log levels, no System.out</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡Use EnumSet for enum values in sets. EnumSet, a Set interface implementation, is specialized for enums, offering memory efficiency and speed via bit-vector representation. ✅ Example usage: EnumSet<Month> months = EnumSet.range(Month.JANUARY, Month.DECEMBER); System.out.println(months); #Java #ProgrammingTips

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

}
body {

  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;

.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// ❌ A NullPointerException waiting to happen</span>
<span class="typ">User</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">find</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getEmail</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="com">// ✅ Make &quot;maybe absent&quot; explicit</span>
<span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">map</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getEmail</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">orElse</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;no-email&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip #20💡: Always check for null values or use 'Optional' to avoid null pointer exceptions. #Java #NullSafety

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 💡Java Tip: Avoid magic numbers. In Java, a Magic Number refers to a hard-coded numeric value used in code without any explanation of what it represents.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// Magic numbers reduce readability and make maintenance harder.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #CleanCode</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡Java Tip: Avoid magic numbers. In Java, a Magic Number refers to a hard-coded numeric value used in code without any explanation of what it represents. Magic numbers reduce readability and make maintenance harder. #Java #CleanCode

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {

  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}

  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;

  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 💡 Java tip: Use interface default methods to handle backward compatibility (starting from Java 8).</span>

html, body {

  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);

.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;

  overflow: hidden;

  display: flex;

<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">supplyAsync</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">fetchUser</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">thenApply</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getName</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">thenAccept</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">System</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="txt">out</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">println</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java Tip 💡: Prefer immutable objects to ensure thread safety. #Java #Concurrency</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip 💡: Prefer immutable objects to ensure thread safety. #Java #Concurrency

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;

  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java Tip 💡: When possible, avoid returning mutable internal collections from methods, so that:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// 1) You don&#x27;t expose internal state, improving security</span>
<span class="com">// 2) You can change the implementation without breaking other parts</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip 💡: When possible, avoid returning mutable internal collections from methods, so that: 1) You don't expose internal state, improving security 2) You can change the implementation without breaking other parts #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java Tip 💡: Try to avoid static variables unless they store truly global values.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// • They hinder testing by making it harder to isolate classes due to shared state.</span>
<span class="com">// • They reduce code reusability. For example:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// public class Config {</span>
<span class="com">//     ...</span>
<span class="com">//     public static String getConfigPath() {</span>
<span class="com">//         return configPath;</span>
<span class="com">//     }</span>
<span class="com">// }</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// The configuration path is shared globally, and changing it in one place could affect others.</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip 💡: Try to avoid static variables unless they store truly global values. • They hinder testing by making it harder to isolate classes due to shared state. • They reduce code reusability. For example: public class Config { ... public static String getConfigPath() { return configPath; } } The configuration path is shared globally, and changing it in one place could affect others.

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Immutable, null-hostile, concise</span>
<span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">roles</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;ADMIN&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;USER&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Integer</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">limits</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;free&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="num">10</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;pro&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="num">100</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="com">// roles.add(&quot;X&quot;) -&gt; UnsupportedOperationException</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip 💡: You can use List.of() and Set.of() to create immutable collections. These are a quick ways to create unmodifiable lists and sets without using Collections.unmodifiableList(). #Java #Collections

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);

  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java tip💡: Avoid using `Thread.sleep()` for timing. Use proper synchronization or schedulers. #Java #Concurrency</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip💡: Avoid using `Thread.sleep()` for timing. Use proper synchronization or schedulers. #Java #Concurrency

Tweet #93

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java Tip 💡: Understand the difference between `==` and `.equals()` for object comparison. #Java #Basics</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip 💡: Understand the difference between `==` and `.equals()` for object comparison. #Java #Basics

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// ❌ A NullPointerException waiting to happen</span>
<span class="typ">User</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">find</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getEmail</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="com">// ✅ Make &quot;maybe absent&quot; explicit</span>
<span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">map</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getEmail</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">orElse</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;no-email&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip 💡: Use `Optional.ifPresentOrElse()` for elegant null handling (Java 9+). #Java #Optional

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }

.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;

  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;

  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }

.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }

  flex-direction: row;

  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;

  flex: 1;

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Worker</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="com">// Without volatile a thread may read a stale, cached value</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">private</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">volatile</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">boolean</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">running</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">true</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">void</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">stop</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">running</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">false</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">void</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">run</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="kw">while</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">running</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">            </span><span class="com">// sees stop() called from another thread</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip💡: Use 'volatile' to declare simple flags shared across threads. #Java #Multithreading

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Compile once and reuse — not on every call</span>
<span class="kw">private</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">static</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Pattern</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">EMAIL</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">Pattern</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">compile</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;^[^@\\s]+@[^@\\s]+$&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="kw">boolean</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">ok</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">EMAIL</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">matcher</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">input</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">matches</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Tweet #104

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java Tip 💡: Avoid initializing objects you may never use. Lazy-load when appropriate. #Java #Performance</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip 💡: Avoid initializing objects you may never use. Lazy-load when appropriate. #Java #Performance

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  box-sizing: border-box;

body {

  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;

.carbon {
  flex: 1;

<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">Comparator</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">comparing</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getLastName</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">              </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">thenComparing</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getFirstName</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;

  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;

  tab-size: 4;

  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;

}

  flex: 1;

  line-height: 24px;
}

  width: 1px;

}

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);

.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// ❌ A new String object on every iteration</span>
<span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">csv</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="kw">for</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">item</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">items</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">csv</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">+</span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">item</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">+</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;,&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="com">// ✅ One mutable buffer — no garbage</span>
<span class="typ">StringBuilder</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">sb</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">new</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">StringBuilder</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="kw">for</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">item</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">items</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">sb</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">append</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">item</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">append</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&#x27;,&#x27;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">csv</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">sb</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">toString</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip💡: Avoid premature optimization. Focus on clean, working code first. #Java #BestPractices Java tip💡: Avoid premature optimization. Write clean, working code first and focus on functional requirements. #Java #BestPractices 🧵1/3 A very trivial example could be: String helloThere = "Hello " + "there" + "!"; System.out.println(helloThere); The above line of code is clear and works as intended. 🧵2/3 In a later time you can think of optimizing it using StringBuilder: StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.append("Hello "); sb.append("there"); sb.append("!"); System.out.println(sb.toString());

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">record</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">SignupRequest</span><span class="pun">(</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="ann">@NotBlank</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">name</span><span class="pun">,</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="ann">@Email</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">email</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="ann">@PostMapping</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;/signup&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">void</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">signup</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="ann">@Valid</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="ann">@RequestBody</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">SignupRequest</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">req</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">service</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">register</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">req</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Arrow form: no fall-through, returns a value</span>
<span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">label</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">switch</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">status</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">case</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">ACTIVE</span><span class="txt">  </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;running&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">case</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">PAUSED</span><span class="txt">  </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;on hold&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">case</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">STOPPED</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;done&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip 💡: Refactor large switch statements into polymorphic classes. #Java #Refactoring

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java Tip 💡: Master the SOLID principles to write cleaner, more maintainable object-oriented code.</span>
<span class="com">// Each letter stands for a key design principle:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// S: Single Responsibility – A class should have only one reason to change.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// O: Open/Closed – Classes should be open for extension, but closed for modification.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// L: Liskov Substitution – Subtypes should be replaceable for their base types without breaking behavior.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// I: Interface Segregation – Prefer many small, specific interfaces over one large general-purpose interface.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// D: Dependency Inversion – Depend on abstractions, not concrete implementations.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// Applying SOLID improves code flexibility, testability, and scalability.</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #CleanCode #OOP #SoftwareDesign</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip 💡: Master the SOLID principles to write cleaner, more maintainable object-oriented code. Each letter stands for a key design principle: S: Single Responsibility – A class should have only one reason to change. O: Open/Closed – Classes should be open for extension, but closed for modification. L: Liskov Substitution – Subtypes should be replaceable for their base types without breaking behavior. I: Interface Segregation – Prefer many small, specific interfaces over one large general-purpose interface. D: Dependency Inversion – Depend on abstractions, not concrete implementations. Applying SOLID improves code flexibility, testability, and scalability. #Java #CleanCode #OOP #SoftwareDesign

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">Order</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Customer</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="com">// Fail fast at construction with a clear message</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">this</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Objects</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">requireNonNull</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;customer&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip 💡: With Map objects, instead of manual null checks, you can use Map.getOrDefault(). Map<String, Integer> map = new HashMap<>(); Integer discount = map.getOrDefault("item1", 0);

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java
