html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);

.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java Tip : To populate Map values, like lists, you can use computeIfAbsent(). #Java #Collections</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java tip 💡:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// If you want to count the occurrences of an element in a list, you can use Collections.frequency(...).</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #Collections</span></code></pre></div></div>

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Immutable, null-hostile, concise</span>
<span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">roles</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;ADMIN&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;USER&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Integer</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">limits</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;free&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="num">10</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;pro&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="num">100</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="com">// roles.add(&quot;X&quot;) -&gt; UnsupportedOperationException</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip 💡: Instead of using HashSet for enum types, consider using EnumSet, it's faster and more memory-efficient. ✅Example: EnumSet<Month> months = EnumSet.of(Month.JANUARY, Month.APRIL); #Java #Collections

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Java tip💡: If you have a list of elements, you want to remove duplicates, and sort them in natural order or by a custom comparator, you can use TreeSet. #Java #Collections

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Immutable, null-hostile, concise</span>
<span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">roles</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;ADMIN&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;USER&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Integer</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">limits</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;free&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="num">10</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;pro&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="num">100</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="com">// roles.add(&quot;X&quot;) -&gt; UnsupportedOperationException</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip 💡: Return Collections.emptyList(), List.of() or Set.of() instead of null collections to avoid null checks in calling code. #JavaBestPractice

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;

  flex-shrink: 0;
}

  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java tip 💡: Try to avoid sharing data across threads.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅Use immutable objects</span>
<span class="com">// ✅Or make threads communicate through messages, without directly modifying shared state.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #Concurrency</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip 💡: Try to avoid sharing data across threads. ✅Use immutable objects ✅Or make threads communicate through messages, without directly modifying shared state. #Java #Concurrency

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java Tip 💡: Override hashCode() when overriding equals() to ensure collections like HashMap work correctly.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// HashMap is based on an hash table data structure, and uses hashCode() to determine the bucket index.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #Collections</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java Tip 💡: Override hashCode() when overriding equals() to ensure collections like HashMap work correctly. HashMap is based on an hash table data structure, and uses hashCode() to determine the bucket index. #Java #Collections

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 💡Java tip: Starting from Java 8,  for parallel processing of collections you can use parallelStream().</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Uses multiple threads</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Good for CPU-intensive processing</span>
<span class="com">// ⚠️ Sharing mutable state is not safe</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡Java tip: Starting from Java 8, for parallel processing of collections you can use parallelStream(). ✅ Uses multiple threads ✅ Good for CPU-intensive processing ⚠️ Sharing mutable state is not safe #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;

body {

  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {

  display: flex;

  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">Order</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Customer</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="com">// Fail fast at construction with a clear message</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">this</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Objects</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">requireNonNull</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;customer&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Java tip: Use Objects.requireNonNullElse(value, defaultValue) to avoid null checks. #Java #CodeTips #NullSafety

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;

  display: flex;

.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Open/Closed Principle (OCP): OCP is a SOLID principle that states classes should be open for extension but closed for modification.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅Instead of touching existing implementation, you should use Java interfaces and inheritance to add new behavior.</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Open/Closed Principle (OCP): OCP is a SOLID principle that states classes should be open for extension but closed for modification. ✅Instead of touching existing implementation, you should use Java interfaces and inheritance to add new behavior. #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }

.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}

  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }

.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;

}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {

  min-width: 0;

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 💡SOLID principles: The Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP) states that objects of a superclass should be replaceable with objects of a subclass.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Subclasses should not behave differently than the base class.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ❌ Example that breaks LSP:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// class Vehicle {</span>
<span class="com">//     public void startEngine() {</span>
<span class="com">//         ...</span>
<span class="com">//     }</span>
<span class="com">// }</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// class Bicycle extends Vehicle {</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// @Override</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">//     public void startEngine() {</span>
<span class="com">//         throw new UnsupportedOperationException(&quot;Bicycles don&#x27;t have engines!&quot;);</span>
<span class="com">//     }</span>
<span class="com">// }</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// If you write a test for the Vehicle class, you would expect every subclass could start the engine. But as we&#x27;ve seen, Bicycle can&#x27;t, it has no engine and throws an Exception.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// This violates LSP. To fix it, we need to change the design so that only appropriate classes have the startEngine() behavior.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ We can for example define two separate interfaces:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// interface Vehicle {...}</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// interface Motorized {</span>
<span class="com">//     void startEngine();</span>
<span class="com">// }</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// class Car implements Vehicle, Motorized {</span>
<span class="com">//     //Vehicle methods implementations</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">//      //Motorized implementation</span>
<span class="com">//     public void startEngine() {</span>
<span class="com">//          ...</span>
<span class="com">//     }</span>
<span class="com">// }</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// class Bicycle implements Vehicle {</span>
<span class="com">//     // No engine, it implements only Vehicle</span>
<span class="com">// }</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ An alternative could be creating a default Vehicle implementation, and extending it while implementing the Motorized interface only for vehicles that have engines.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #SOLID #SoftwareDesign</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡SOLID principles: The Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP) states that objects of a superclass should be replaceable with objects of a subclass. ✅ Subclasses should not behave differently than the base class. ❌ Example that breaks LSP: class Vehicle { public void startEngine() { ... } } class Bicycle extends Vehicle { @Override public void startEngine() { throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Bicycles don't have engines!"); } } If you write a test for the Vehicle class, you would expect every subclass could start the engine. But as we've seen, Bicycle can't, it has no engine and throws an Exception. This violates LSP. To fix it, we need to change the design so that only appropriate classes have the startEngine() behavior. ✅ We can for example define two separate interfaces: interface Vehicle {...} interface Motorized { void startEngine(); } class Car implements Vehicle, Motorized { //Vehicle methods implementations //Motorized implementation public void startEngine() { ... } } class Bicycle implements Vehicle { // No engine, it implements only Vehicle } ✅ An alternative could be creating a default Vehicle implementation, and extending it while implementing the Motorized interface only for vehicles that have engines. #SOLID #SoftwareDesign

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;

}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;

}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 💡 SOLID Principles: Interface Segregation Principle (ISP) states that clients shouldn’t depend on methods they don’t use.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ So called &quot;fat&quot;interfaces (large, general-purpose interfaces) should be broken down into smaller, more specific ones. Classes shouldn&#x27;t be forced to implement methods that are irrelevant to them.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 SOLID Principles: Interface Segregation Principle (ISP) states that clients shouldn’t depend on methods they don’t use. ✅ So called "fat"interfaces (large, general-purpose interfaces) should be broken down into smaller, more specific ones. Classes shouldn't be forced to implement methods that are irrelevant to them. #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<span class="com">// #SOLID #SoftwareDesign</span></code></pre></div></div>

  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);

}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }

.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }

.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }

  flex-direction: row;

  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Arrow form: no fall-through, returns a value</span>
<span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">label</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">switch</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">status</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">case</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">ACTIVE</span><span class="txt">  </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;running&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">case</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">PAUSED</span><span class="txt">  </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;on hold&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">case</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">STOPPED</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;done&quot;</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡Java tip: Starting from Java 14 you can use switch expressions for shorter and cleaner multi-branch logic: #Java #ModernJava

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #143

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">Dept</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">byDept</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">employees</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">collect</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Collectors</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">groupingBy</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getDept</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="kw">long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">active</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">employees</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">filter</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">isActive</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">count</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip: Collectors.toMap() will throw an error if your keys collide. Always consider a merge function. #Java #Streams Example: ❌ Throws: IllegalStateException: Duplicate key: List<Integer> nums = Arrays.asList(1, 2, 3, 4); Map<Integer, Integer> squares = nums.stream() .collect(Collectors.toMap( n -> n % 2, n -> n * n )); ✅ Fix: merge duplicates by summing values: Map<Integer, Integer> squaresMerged = nums.stream() .collect(Collectors.toMap( n -> n % 2, n -> n * n, Integer::sum ));

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@Cacheable</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;users&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">orElseThrow</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="com">// Enable with @EnableCaching</span></code></pre></div></div>

💡Java tip: Use WeakHashMap for caching when the unreferenced keys should be garbage-collected. #Java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;

  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">lines</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">readAllLines</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;data.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">writeString</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;out.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;hello&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Java tip: Use Files.readString() and Files.writeString() for straightforward file I/O. #Java

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #148

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 💡 Java tip: Avoid boxing overhead by using primitive streams.</span>
<span class="com">// #Java</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Java tip: Avoid boxing overhead by using primitive streams. #Java

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;

  overflow: auto;

  tab-size: 4;

.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Type is still static — just inferred by the compiler</span>
<span class="kw">var</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">users</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">new</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">ArrayList</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="kw">var</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">entry</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">entry</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;k&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="num">1</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="kw">for</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="kw">var</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">users</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">System</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="txt">out</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">println</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getName</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Java tip: Use var for local variables only when the type is obvious from the right-hand side or when the generic type is too verbose. #Java #JavaTips

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">private</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">static</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Logger</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">log</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">LoggerFactory</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getLogger</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">OrderService</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="txt">log</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">info</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;Created order {} for {}&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">orderId</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="com">// Lazy formatting, log levels, no System.out</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip: Starting from Java 11, if you need to repeat n times a specific string, you can use the String method repeat(n). Example: String result = "$".repeat(10); System.out.println(result); // $$$$$$$$$$ #Java #JavaTips

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">names</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">users</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">filter</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">isActive</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">map</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getName</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">sorted</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">toList</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="com">// Declarative: say WHAT you want, not HOW to loop</span></code></pre></div></div>

Java tip: Starting from java 9 you can use Stream.takeWhile() for easy slicing of lists by streams. #Java #JavaTips

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">Dept</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">byDept</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">employees</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">collect</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Collectors</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">groupingBy</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getDept</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="kw">long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">active</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">employees</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">filter</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">isActive</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">count</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Java tip: You can use Collectors.joining(", ") to turn a stream into a comma-separated string (or another custom separator). #Java #JavaTips

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">private</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">static</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Logger</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">log</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">LoggerFactory</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getLogger</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">OrderService</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="txt">log</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">info</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;Created order {} for {}&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">orderId</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="com">// Lazy formatting, log levels, no System.out</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip : Avoid static variables unless they store truly global values. They hinder testing by making it harder to isolate classes due to shared state. It can cause subtle bugs. For example: public class BankAccount { public static double balance; public BankAccount(double initialBalance) { balance = initialBalance; } public void deposit(double amount) { balance += amount; } public void printBalance() { System.out.println("Balance: " + balance); } } BankAccount acc1 = new BankAccount(1000); BankAccount acc2 = new BankAccount(500); acc1.deposit(200); acc2.printBalance(); // Balance: 700 (should be 500) The balance variable is shared globally, and changing it in one object affects the others. #Java #JavaTips

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Code — CodePen pen

  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;

.carbon {

  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java tip: Use List.copyOf(list) for a safe, unmodifiable copy, while avoiding accidental changes.  #Java #JavaTips</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java tip: Use List.copyOf(list) for a safe, unmodifiable copy, while avoiding accidental changes. #Java #JavaTips

  box-sizing: border-box;

💡 Java tip: Stream.toArray(Type[]::new) is a clean, type-safe way to get arrays from streams. ✅ Avoids Object[] and casting. #Java #JavaDev

  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 💡 Java tip: Starting from Java 8 you can use ChronoUnit.between(start, end) to calculate time intervals in a specific unit (days, hours, minutes) without manual math.</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">Dept</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">byDept</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">employees</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">collect</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Collectors</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">groupingBy</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getDept</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="kw">long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">active</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">employees</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">filter</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">isActive</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">count</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡Java tip: Use Collectors.groupingBy(...) to group stream results by a classifier. #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">names</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">users</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">filter</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">isActive</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">map</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getName</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">sorted</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">toList</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="com">// Declarative: say WHAT you want, not HOW to loop</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Java tip: Use Stream.peek() for debugging in pipelines, not for business logic. ✅ Stream.peek() lets you perform logging or inspection on each element as it flows through a stream without modifying it. #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java
