.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// ❌ A NullPointerException waiting to happen</span>
<span class="typ">User</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">find</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getEmail</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="com">// ✅ Make &quot;maybe absent&quot; explicit</span>
<span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">map</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getEmail</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">orElse</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;no-email&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Java tip: Use Optional.filter(...) to add inline conditions to an Optional object. ✅ It lets you apply a condition directly and it returns the original Optional if the predicate passes, an empty one otherwise. #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #170

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">CompletableFuture</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">supplyAsync</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">fetchUser</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">thenApply</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getName</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">thenAccept</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">System</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="txt">out</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">println</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Java: With CompletableFuture.allOf(...) you can wait on multiple async tasks without manual join handling. #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

}
body {

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">Map</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">Dept</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">byDept</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">employees</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">collect</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Collectors</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">groupingBy</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getDept</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="kw">long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">active</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">employees</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">filter</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Employee</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">isActive</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">count</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">lines</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">readAllLines</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;data.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">writeString</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;out.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;hello&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Use Files.isSameFile(path1, path2) to check if two paths refer to the same file. It resolves symlinks too. ✅ It checks whether two Path objects refer to the same actual file in the file system. #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// ❌ Type checks scattered everywhere</span>
<span class="kw">if</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">instanceof</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Dog</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">d</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">d</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">bark</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="kw">else</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">if</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">animal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">instanceof</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Cat</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">c</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">c</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">meow</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="com">// ✅ Let polymorphism decide</span>
<span class="txt">animal</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">makeSound</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡Java tip: With pattern matching for the instanceof operator, Java lets you declare a variable directly inside the instanceof check. #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #175

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// ⌛️⏲️ Java tip: Instant.truncatedTo(...) is useful for comparing times at minute/hour/day precision.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ It avoids equality checks failing due to overly precise values.</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #SoftwareEngineering</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

⌛️⏲️ Java tip: Instant.truncatedTo(...) is useful for comparing times at minute/hour/day precision. ✅ It avoids equality checks failing due to overly precise values. #Java #SoftwareEngineering

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="txt">users</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">sort</span><span class="pun">(</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">Comparator</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">comparing</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getLastName</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">              </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">thenComparing</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getFirstName</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Java: Use Comparator.nullsFirst() or Comparator.nullsLast() for safe sorting. 🔴 Null values can cause a NullPointerException if your comparator doesn’t explicitly handle them. #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;

  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}

  border-radius: 10px;

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Java: Objects.equals(a, b) is a choice for null-safe comparisons.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ If both are null, returns true</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ If one is null, returns false</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ If neither is null, calls a.equals(b)</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Java: Objects.equals(a, b) is a choice for null-safe comparisons. ✅ If both are null, returns true ✅ If one is null, returns false ✅ If neither is null, calls a.equals(b) #Java #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Compile once and reuse — not on every call</span>
<span class="kw">private</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">static</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Pattern</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">EMAIL</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">Pattern</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">compile</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;^[^@\\s]+@[^@\\s]+$&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="kw">boolean</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">ok</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">EMAIL</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">matcher</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">input</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">matches</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Java: A very interesting project by https://github.com/Mirkoddd, named Sift, replaces the cryptic, string-based approach of the Java Regex engine with a fluent API. #Java #JavaDev 🧵A link to the GitHub project to follow

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #180

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">lines</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">readAllLines</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;data.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">writeString</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;out.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;hello&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡Java: If you have to read small files, you can use Files.readAllLines(), it's more direct and simple. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}

  flex: 1;

  background: #011627;

  overflow: auto;

.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;

.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }

.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;

.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;

  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// ❌ A NullPointerException waiting to happen</span>
<span class="typ">User</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">find</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">u</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getEmail</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="com">// ✅ Make &quot;maybe absent&quot; explicit</span>
<span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">map</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getEmail</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">orElse</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;no-email&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Java: You can use Optional.orElseThrow() to implement so called fail-fast behavior. ✅ Fail-fast means you can catch bugs closer to their source, making stack traces easier to understand #Java #SoftwareDevelopment

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">java-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">names</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">users</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">stream</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">filter</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">isActive</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">map</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt">getName</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">sorted</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">toList</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="com">// Declarative: say WHAT you want, not HOW to loop</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Java: You can use Stream.limit(n) to set a bound to infinite streams. #Java #SoftwareDevelopment

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #185

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// @RestController = @Controller + @ResponseBody</span>
<span class="ann">@RestController</span>
<span class="ann">@RequestMapping</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;/api&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">UserController</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>

<span class="txt">    </span><span class="ann">@GetMapping</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;/users&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">all</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">service</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">findAll</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt">   </span><span class="com">// serialized to JSON</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Spring Boot: To write cleaner REST controllers, use @RestController instead of @Controller + @ResponseBody #SpringBoot #CleanCode

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@Bean</span>
<span class="typ">SecurityFilterChain</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">security</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">HttpSecurity</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">http</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">throws</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Exception</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">http</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">authorizeHttpRequests</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">a</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">a</span>
<span class="txt">            </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">requestMatchers</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;/public/**&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">permitAll</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">            </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">anyRequest</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">authenticated</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">httpBasic</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Customizer</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">withDefaults</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">build</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

<span class="ann">@Profile</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;prod&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="typ">DataSource</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">prodDataSource</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">new</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">PgDataSource</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

<span class="txt">        </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">authorizeHttpRequests</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">a</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">a</span>

<span class="txt">        </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">build</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Ignore unknown JSON fields instead of failing</span>
<span class="ann">@JsonIgnoreProperties</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">ignoreUnknown</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">true</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">record</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">UserDto</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">name</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">email</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Spring Boot Tip: If you want DTOs to ignore extra JSON fields from clients without breaking your API use @JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown = true). #SpringBoot #CodingTips #SoftwareEngineering

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 💡 Spring Boot tip: Spring Boot prints a banner in the console when your app starts. You can customize it for branding or just fun.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Add a file banner.txt  in src/main/resources and put whatever content you want.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ You can also use placeholders like ${spring-boot.version} or ${java.version} to inject values inside it.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ If you want, you can disable the banner with the following configuration property:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// spring.main.banner-mode=off</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #SpringBoot</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Spring Boot tip: Spring Boot prints a banner in the console when your app starts. You can customize it for branding or just fun. ✅ Add a file banner.txt in src/main/resources and put whatever content you want. ✅ You can also use placeholders like ${spring-boot.version} or ${java.version} to inject values inside it. ✅ If you want, you can disable the banner with the following configuration property: spring.main.banner-mode=off #Java #SpringBoot

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;

}

  flex-direction: row;

  min-width: 0;

  flex-direction: column;

  flex: 1;

  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;

.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";

  top: 50%;

  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;

  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;

  color: #82aaff;

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@Scheduled</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">fixedRate</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="num">5000</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">void</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">poll</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">log</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">info</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;tick&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="com">// Enable with @EnableScheduling on a @Configuration class</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Spring Boot tip: You can run jobs at fixed intervals without setting up Quartz or external schedulers. #Java #SpringBoot

🚀Spring Boot DevTools is a tool to ease developer's work a bit. It watches code and configuration for changes, then automatically reloads or restarts your app. #SpringBoot #SoftwareDevelopment

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot tip: In development, you can package and run an application from the source code with just one command:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ When you’re in development, you can skip packaging and just run directly:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ./mvnw spring-boot:run</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ If you want, you can combine packaging and run in one command:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ./mvnw clean package &amp;&amp; java -jar target/myapp-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #Java #SpringBoot</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot tip: In development, you can package and run an application from the source code with just one command: ✅ When you’re in development, you can skip packaging and just run directly: ./mvnw spring-boot:run ✅ If you want, you can combine packaging and run in one command: ./mvnw clean package && java -jar target/myapp-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar #Java #SpringBoot

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);

.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀Spring Boot: Use TaskScheduler to implement lightweight dynamic tasks (consider Quartz for more complex requirements).</span>

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@Bean</span>
<span class="typ">CommandLineRunner</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">init</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">UserRepository</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">args</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">save</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="kw">new</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">User</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;Mario&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="com">// Runs once, right after the context starts</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Spring Boot tip: Use CommandLineRunner for executing startup logic. CommandLineRunner is a Spring Boot interface that can be implemented to run code after the application context is fully initialized. #SpringBoot #Java

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">private</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">static</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Logger</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">log</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">LoggerFactory</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getLogger</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">OrderService</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="txt">log</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">info</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;Created order {} for {}&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">orderId</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="com">// Lazy formatting, log levels, no System.out</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Spring Boot tip: Use spring-boot-starter-logging (Logback) for logging, it is already included as default. ✅ spring-boot-starter-logging is already included in most starters (spring-boot-starter-web, spring-boot-starter-data-jpa, etc.) ✅ You can customize log levels by editing application.yml: logging: level: root: INFO com.myapp: DEBUG ✅ To customize format and rotation, create a logback-spring.xml in src/main/resources. #SpringBoot #Java

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@Cacheable</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;users&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">orElseThrow</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="com">// Enable with @EnableCaching</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀Spring Boot tip: Boost performance in Spring Boot with @EnableCaching and @Cacheable . 🟢 Avoids repeated DB/API calls 🟢 Reduces latency 🟢 By default, Spring uses an in-memory map, but you can plug in an external provider for production. #SpringBoot #Performance

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@Transactional</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">void</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">transfer</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">from</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">to</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">BigDecimal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">amount</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">accounts</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">debit</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">from</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">amount</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">accounts</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">credit</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">to</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">amount</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt">   </span><span class="com">// both commit, or both roll back</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Spring Boot tip: use @Transactional at the service layer, not on repositories. ✅ You coordinate business logic in the service layer, and a service method: 🟢 Calls one or more repositories 🟢 Implements business rules 🟢 Needs everything to succeed or fail together #SpringBoot

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #200

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">WebClient</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">client</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">WebClient</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">create</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;https://api.example.com&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="typ">Mono</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">user</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">client</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">get</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">uri</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;/users/{id}&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">retrieve</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">bodyToMono</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Spring Boot tip: Use @SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = RANDOM_PORT) for integration tests when testing controllers. ✅ Starts a real embedded server ✅ The server runs on a random port. ✅ You can test controllers via TestRestTemplate or WebTestClient. #SpringBoot

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: springdoc-openapi automatically generates OpenAPI 3.0 (Swagger) documentation from your Spring Boot REST controllers.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// You will get:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ An interactive Swagger UI</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Auto-scanned from your code</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Export API docs in JSON/YAML</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: springdoc-openapi automatically generates OpenAPI 3.0 (Swagger) documentation from your Spring Boot REST controllers. You will get: ✅ An interactive Swagger UI ✅ Auto-scanned from your code ✅ Export API docs in JSON/YAML #SpringBoot

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: You can enable a smooth shutdown with &quot;server.shutdown=graceful&quot; in your configuration.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅  It helps you avoid these situations:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// 🔴 Active HTTP requests failing abruptly</span>
<span class="com">// 🔴 Database rolling back unexpectedly</span>
<span class="com">// 🔴 Threads interrupted before finishing</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: You can enable a smooth shutdown with "server.shutdown=graceful" in your configuration. ✅ It helps you avoid these situations: 🔴 Active HTTP requests failing abruptly 🔴 Database rolling back unexpectedly 🔴 Threads interrupted before finishing #SpringBoot

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

💡 Pin the Spring Boot version in the spring-boot-dependencies BOM to ensure consistent dependency management. ✅ The spring-boot-dependencies BOM allows you to omit versions of libraries that Spring Boot integrates with in your pom.xml. #SpringBoot

🚀 Spring Boot: Use ResponseEntity<T> to get fine-grained control over HTTP responses. You can explicitly specify Status, Headers and Body #SpringBoot

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀  Spring Boot: Use spring-boot-starter-mail for sending emails.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ It wraps all the necessary configuration and dependencies, making the process of sending emails simple and clear.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: Use spring-boot-starter-mail for sending emails. ✅ It wraps all the necessary configuration and dependencies, making the process of sending emails simple and clear. #SpringBoot

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: With @DataJpaTest you can test JPA repositories in isolation.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// @DataJpaTest only loads only the JPA layer and not the entire application context, using an in-memory database (H2), and rolls back transactions after each test.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #Java</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: With @DataJpaTest you can test JPA repositories in isolation. @DataJpaTest only loads only the JPA layer and not the entire application context, using an in-memory database (H2), and rolls back transactions after each test. #SpringBoot #Java

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 💡 Spring Boot: To better handle circular dependencies, use constructor injection instead of field injection.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Forces all required dependencies to be provided at creation time, allowing early detection.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡 Spring Boot: To better handle circular dependencies, use constructor injection instead of field injection. ✅ Forces all required dependencies to be provided at creation time, allowing early detection. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ One possible reason could be that you have a custom configuration that conflicts with the default.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: Use @SpringBootApplication(exclude = …) to disable specific auto-configuration classes. ✅ One possible reason could be that you have a custom configuration that conflicts with the default. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@ConfigurationProperties</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">prefix</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;app&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">record</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">AppProps</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">name</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">int</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">timeout</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="com">// Binds app.name / app.timeout from application.yaml</span></code></pre></div></div>

🚀 Spring Boot: If you need to inject grouped configuration properties into classes, you can use @ConfigurationProperties instead of @Value #SpringBoot #JavaDev

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}

  display: flex;

  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: In development phase, use spring.main.lazy-initialization=true to speed up the startup of the application.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

🚀 Spring Boot: In development phase, use spring.main.lazy-initialization=true to speed up the startup of the application. #SpringBoot #JavaDev
