Tweet #211

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">record</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">SignupRequest</span><span class="pun">(</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="ann">@NotBlank</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">name</span><span class="pun">,</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="ann">@Email</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">email</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span><span class="pun">}</span>

<span class="ann">@PostMapping</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;/signup&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">void</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">signup</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="ann">@Valid</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="ann">@RequestBody</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">SignupRequest</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">req</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">service</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">register</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">req</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: You can validate your input DTO objects with @Valid + @NotBlank, @Email, etc. #Java #SoftwareDevelopment

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

💡Spring Boot tip: If you need to implement integrations tests for Spring MVC controllers, and mock everything else, use @WebMvcTest to load only MVC-related beans. #SpringBoot #IntegrationTesting

  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">WebClient</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">client</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">WebClient</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">create</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;https://api.example.com&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="typ">Mono</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">user</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">client</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">get</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">uri</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;/users/{id}&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">retrieve</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">bodyToMono</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀Spring Boot: You have the choice of using WebClient over RestTemplate in new apps. ✅ It’s reactive and non-blocking ✅ Based on event-loop instead of thread-per-request ✅ Especially fitted for microservices #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #217

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 SpringBoot: Add global OpenAPI metadata with @OpenAPIDefinition.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ It should be put on your main Spring Boot application class, or a dedicated configuration class</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 SpringBoot: Add global OpenAPI metadata with @OpenAPIDefinition. ✅ It should be put on your main Spring Boot application class, or a dedicated configuration class #SpringBoot #JavaDev

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@Cacheable</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;users&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">repo</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">findById</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">orElseThrow</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="com">// Enable with @EnableCaching</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: You can cache expensive API calls using @Cacheable and Redis. ✅First request with a specific key: real API call ✅Subsequent requests: Redis (milliseconds) #SpringBoot #JavaDev 🧵Spring Boot demo link to follow:

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">WebClient</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">client</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">WebClient</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">create</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;https://api.example.com&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="typ">Mono</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">user</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">client</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">get</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">uri</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;/users/{id}&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">id</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">retrieve</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">bodyToMono</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">User</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: If your code uses RestTemplate, you can implement tests by mocking external calls with MockRestServiceServer. ✅ MockRestServiceServer works by intercepting HTTP requests #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #222

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: To make Jackson create your objects from JSON in an explicit, and predictable way, you can use @JsonCreator.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// 🟢 This way, each parameter is explicitly bound</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: To make Jackson create your objects from JSON in an explicit, and predictable way, you can use @JsonCreator. 🟢 This way, each parameter is explicitly bound #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: Better avoid wildcard exclusions for transitive dependencies in POMs, because they can hide problems.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ❌ Wildcard exclusions just remove the symptom and mask the underlying version mismatch</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: Better avoid wildcard exclusions for transitive dependencies in POMs, because they can hide problems. ❌ Wildcard exclusions just remove the symptom and mask the underlying version mismatch #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}

  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;

.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: Keep controllers thin, put logic in services.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Controllers main job is just to handle HTTP concerns</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #SoftwareEngineering</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: Keep controllers thin, put logic in services. ✅ Controllers main job is just to handle HTTP concerns #SpringBoot #SoftwareEngineering

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: Avoid FetchType.EAGER unless you really need it.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ❌ Marking a relationship as EAGER tells the ORM to load it whenever the entity is loaded, regardless of whether you actually invoke the relationship&#x27;s method.</span>
<span class="com">// #Springboot #SoftwareDevelopment</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: Avoid FetchType.EAGER unless you really need it. ❌ Marking a relationship as EAGER tells the ORM to load it whenever the entity is loaded, regardless of whether you actually invoke the relationship's method. #Springboot #SoftwareDevelopment

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  box-sizing: border-box;

}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);

  border-radius: 10px;

}

.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: You can implement optimistic locking using the @Version annotation.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Ideal when:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// Reads are frequent</span>
<span class="com">// Writes are relatively rare</span>
<span class="com">// Conflicts are uncommon</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ❌ Not Ideal when:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// Conflicts are frequent</span>
<span class="com">// Strict serialization is needed</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: You can implement optimistic locking using the @Version annotation. ✅ Ideal when: Reads are frequent Writes are relatively rare Conflicts are uncommon ❌ Not Ideal when: Conflicts are frequent Strict serialization is needed #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #SoftwareDevelopment</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: Prefer static utility classes over Spring beans only if they don't hold state, they have no dependencies and they don't need mocking or AOP. #SpringBoot #SoftwareDevelopment

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot:  Better not expose JPA entities directly, use DTOs at boundaries instead.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅This allows to:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// 🟢 Make API independent on the persistence layer</span>
<span class="com">// 🟢 Prevent lazy loading problems</span>
<span class="com">// 🟢 Improve security</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #SoftwareEngineer</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: Better not expose JPA entities directly, use DTOs at boundaries instead. ✅This allows to: 🟢 Make API independent on the persistence layer 🟢 Prevent lazy loading problems 🟢 Improve security #SpringBoot #SoftwareEngineer

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">private</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">static</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">final</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Logger</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">log</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="typ">LoggerFactory</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getLogger</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">OrderService</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>

<span class="txt">log</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">info</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;Created order {} for {}&quot;</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">orderId</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="com">// Lazy formatting, log levels, no System.out</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 You can use async logging in Spring Boot by configuring logback (logback-spring.xml). ✅ Log messages are sent into a queue, and are elaborated by a separate background thread. ✅ This reduces I/O bottlenecks. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #233

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: @AutoConfigureMockMvc allows you to test controllers without starting the server. It tells Spring Boot to automatically configure a MockMvc instance in the test context.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #Java</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: @AutoConfigureMockMvc allows you to test controllers without starting the server. It tells Spring Boot to automatically configure a MockMvc instance in the test context. #SpringBoot #Java

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// Spring Boot: Use spring.mvc.publish-request-params=true only in dev. It&#x27;s dangerous in production.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// 🔴In production, logging request details can expose sensitive information.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

Spring Boot: Use spring.mvc.publish-request-params=true only in dev. It's dangerous in production. 🔴In production, logging request details can expose sensitive information. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: Better keep the default spring.main.allow-bean-definition-overriding=false configuration.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅This way Spring fails to start if two beans share the same name.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅Useful because bean overriding can hide configuration bugs</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: Better keep the default spring.main.allow-bean-definition-overriding=false configuration. ✅This way Spring fails to start if two beans share the same name. ✅Useful because bean overriding can hide configuration bugs #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: Better use global filters/interceptors only for true cross-cutting concerns, and avoid business logic. They are executed for every incoming HTTP request.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: Better use global filters/interceptors only for true cross-cutting concerns, and avoid business logic. They are executed for every incoming HTTP request. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

Spring Boot: Avoid importing the full spring-boot-starter-web if you only need Spring MVC, because the full starter brings in extra components like an embedded server that you may not need. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;

.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: You can use the &quot;spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.jdbc.batch_size&quot; property to make Hibernate handle batch inserts.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// 🟢Fewer network calls</span>
<span class="com">// 🟢Less JDBC overhead</span>
<span class="com">// 🟢Faster bulk operations</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: You can use the "spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.jdbc.batch_size" property to make Hibernate handle batch inserts. 🟢Fewer network calls 🟢Less JDBC overhead 🟢Faster bulk operations #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #239

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="pun">#</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">application</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="txt">yaml</span>
<span class="txt">management</span><span class="pun">:</span>
<span class="txt">  </span><span class="txt">endpoints</span><span class="pun">:</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">web</span><span class="pun">:</span>
<span class="txt">      </span><span class="txt">exposure</span><span class="pun">:</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="txt">include</span><span class="pun">:</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">health</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">info</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">metrics</span>
<span class="pun">#</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">-</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">GET</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">/</span><span class="txt">actuator</span><span class="pun">/</span><span class="txt">health</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: You can enable /actuator/beans to debug missing beans. By hitting the actuator endpoint, you will see information like: ✅ Bean name ✅ Bean type ✅ Bean scope ✅ Bean dependencies #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: A webhook is when another system  sends a POST request to your app when something happens. In Spring Boot you implement it simply exposing an endpoint that receives that POST request.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: A webhook is when another system sends a POST request to your app when something happens. In Spring Boot you implement it simply exposing an endpoint that receives that POST request. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot testing: @MockBean by default resets the mocks between tests, to keep them independent. You can disable this behaviour with @MockBean(reset = MockReset.NONE).</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot testing: @MockBean by default resets the mocks between tests, to keep them independent. You can disable this behaviour with @MockBean(reset = MockReset.NONE). #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;

  display: flex;

  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">Order</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Customer</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="com">// Fail fast at construction with a clear message</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">this</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Objects</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">requireNonNull</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">customer</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;customer&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: You can validate config classes with the @Validated annotation. ✅ It makes your app fail fast at startup if the configuration is invalid. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}

  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}

.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">lines</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">readAllLines</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;data.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">writeString</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;out.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;hello&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Spring Boot: Better avoid logging inside tight loops, since you could affect the application performance and flood log files. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀Spring Boot by default does not include stacktrace in the response of REST service calls. Only for fast debugging purposes (don&#x27;t do it in production), you can enable it with the configuration property include-stacktrace: always.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀Spring Boot by default does not include stacktrace in the response of REST service calls. Only for fast debugging purposes (don't do it in production), you can enable it with the configuration property include-stacktrace: always. #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅Results are fetched in chunks, with only a small subset in memory at any time</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

🚀 Spring Boot: You have the option of using streaming (Stream<T>) for queries with large datasets. ✅Results are fetched in chunks, with only a small subset in memory at any time #SpringBoot #JavaDev

  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@Transactional</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">void</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">transfer</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">from</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Long</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">to</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">BigDecimal</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">amount</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">accounts</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">debit</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">from</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">amount</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="txt">accounts</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">credit</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">to</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">amount</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span><span class="txt">   </span><span class="com">// both commit, or both roll back</span>
<span class="pun">}</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: @TransactionalEventListener can be used to implement post-commit actions. #SpringBoot #JavaDev 🧵A full article with an example to follow

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Tweet #250

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: You can handle CORS globally with a WebMvcConfigurer</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅CORS is the mechanism that allows a web application on one domain (origin) to access resources from a different domain.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #SoftwareDevelopment</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: You can handle CORS globally with a WebMvcConfigurer ✅CORS is the mechanism that allows a web application on one domain (origin) to access resources from a different domain. #SpringBoot #SoftwareDevelopment

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: An efficient way of handling large CSV files is by using Spring Batch features.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// Spring Batch is configurable with options like:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Chunking</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Parallelism</span>
<span class="com">// ✅ Retry</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #SoftwareDevelopment</span></code></pre></div></div>

🚀 Spring Boot: An efficient way of handling large CSV files is by using Spring Batch features. Spring Batch is configurable with options like: ✅ Chunking ✅ Parallelism ✅ Retry #SpringBoot #SoftwareDevelopment

  flex-direction: column;

}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="ann">@RestControllerAdvice</span>
<span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">ApiExceptionHandler</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>

<span class="txt">    </span><span class="ann">@ExceptionHandler</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">NotFoundException</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="kw">class</span><span class="pun">)</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="kw">public</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">ResponseEntity</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="met">handle</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">NotFoundException</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">e</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">{</span>
<span class="txt">        </span><span class="kw">return</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">ResponseEntity</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">status</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="num">404</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">body</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="txt">e</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">getMessage</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="txt">    </span><span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="pun">}</span>
<span class="com">// Controllers stay clean — no try/catch everywhere</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀Spring Boot: You can keep full traceability while handling exceptions in a centralized way, and returning safe API responses with @RestControllerAdvice #SpringBoot #JavaDev

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀Spring Boot: Adding request tracing is very important for debugging APIs</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #JavaDev</span></code></pre></div></div>

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀@Sql or @SqlGroup allows you to preload test data to perform reproducible tests.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// It allows:</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅Insert test data</span>
<span class="com">// ✅Clean up tables</span>
<span class="com">// ✅Reset database state</span>

<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #IntegrationTesting</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀@Sql or @SqlGroup allows you to preload test data to perform reproducible tests. It allows: ✅Insert test data ✅Clean up tables ✅Reset database state #SpringBoot #IntegrationTesting

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);

.carbon {

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="typ">List</span><span class="pun">&lt;</span><span class="typ">String</span><span class="pun">&gt;</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="txt">lines</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="pun">=</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">readAllLines</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;data.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span>
<span class="typ">Files</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">writeString</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="typ">Path</span><span class="pun">.</span><span class="met">of</span><span class="pun">(</span><span class="str">&quot;out.txt&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">,</span><span class="txt"> </span><span class="str">&quot;hello&quot;</span><span class="pun">)</span><span class="pun">;</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀Spring Boot: Using Spring Cloud Config for centralized configuration management is important. ✅Instead of many services each carrying their own application.yml, you pull it from a single source. #SpringCloud #Microservices 🧵1/6 🧵2/6 ✅First of all, you have to configure the config server. ✅You will have a config server app annotated with @EnableConfigServer and a Git repo holding all your .yml files. ✅The server serves configurations over HTTP and clients fetch them at startup. 🧵3/6 ✅The Git repo structure will follow Spring Boot's naming convention: {application-name}-{profile}.yml or {application-name}/{profile}.yml: 🧵4/6 The Client application needs to be set up with the starter dependency and a bit of configuration: 🧵5/6 You can can get dynamic refresh without restart by making beans reloadable with @RefreshScope: 🧵6/6 Sensitive information must be encrypted in the configuration files: #MicroservicesSecurity

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

🚀Spring Boot integrates easily with Kafka to implement event-driven architectures. 🧵1/3 #SpringBoot #JavaDev 🧵2/3 First, you set the required dependency on the pom, then you can configure it in the application.xml, defining how to reach the Kafka cluster, and how consumers and producers are defined. 🧵3/3 The final step is implementing consumer and producers as Java objects:

Tweet #261

Code — CodePen pen

html, body {
  margin: 0;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
body {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
}
.carbon {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  width: 100%;
  min-height: calc(100vh - 1rem);
  max-width: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5);
  overflow: hidden;
}
.carbon-titlebar {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  height: 36px;
  padding: 0 16px;
  background: #252836;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot {
  width: 12px;
  height: 12px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.carbon-dot.red { background: #ff5f56; }
.carbon-dot.yellow { background: #ffbd2e; }
.carbon-dot.green { background: #27c93f; }
.carbon-module {
  margin-left: auto;
  font: 13px/1 system-ui, sans-serif;
  color: #8b949e;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em;
}
.carbon-editor {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  min-height: 0;
  background: #011627;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem 1.75rem;
  overflow: auto;
}
.carbon-editor pre {
  margin: 0;
  flex: 1;
  font-family: "Fira Code", "JetBrains Mono", Consolas, monospace;
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 30px;
  white-space: pre;
  tab-size: 4;
}
.carbon-editor .kw { color: #c792ea; }
.carbon-editor .ann { color: #ffb86c; }
.carbon-editor .str { color: #c3e88d; }
.carbon-editor .typ { color: #ffcb6b; }
.carbon-editor .met { color: #82aaff; }
.carbon-editor .com { color: #5c6370; }
.carbon-editor .num { color: #f78c6c; }
.carbon-editor .pun { color: #89ddff; }
.carbon-editor .txt { color: #d6deeb; }
.carbon-editor.cols {
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 1.75rem;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col {
  flex: 1 1 0;
  min-width: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col pre {
  flex: 1;
  font-size: 15px;
  line-height: 24px;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider {
  align-self: stretch;
  position: relative;
  width: 1px;
  background: #1d3b53;
  flex: 0 0 auto;
}
.carbon-editor.cols .col-divider::after {
  content: "\2192";
  position: absolute;
  top: 50%;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  width: 30px;
  height: 30px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: #011627;
  border: 1px solid #1d3b53;
  color: #82aaff;
  font-size: 16px;
}

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀 Spring Boot: @ConditionalOnClass is a Spring Boot annotation used in auto-configuration.</span>
<span class="com">//</span>
<span class="com">// ✅It conditionally enables a bean or configuration only if a specified class is present on the classpath</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #Microservices</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀 Spring Boot: @ConditionalOnClass is a Spring Boot annotation used in auto-configuration. ✅It conditionally enables a bean or configuration only if a specified class is present on the classpath #SpringBoot #Microservices

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java

  height: 100%;
  min-height: 100vh;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}

  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
  justify-content: stretch;
  padding: 0.5rem;

<div class="carbon"><div class="carbon-titlebar"><span class="carbon-dot red"></span><span class="carbon-dot yellow"></span><span class="carbon-dot green"></span><span class="carbon-module">spring-boot-tips</span></div><div class="carbon-editor"><pre><code><span class="com">// 🚀Spring Boot: spring.main.lazy-initialization=true doesn&#x27;t just delay bean creation, it skips @PostConstruct entirely until first use. Be careful and watch for side effects.</span>
<span class="com">// #SpringBoot #SoftwareDevelopment</span></code></pre></div></div>

Open pen preview · New pen on CodePen

🚀Spring Boot: spring.main.lazy-initialization=true doesn't just delay bean creation, it skips @PostConstruct entirely until first use. Be careful and watch for side effects. #SpringBoot #SoftwareDevelopment

.drawio diagram · CodePen preview · Source .java
